title: artemisia vulgaris var. glandulifera
intro: Artemisia vulgaris var. glandulifera, also known as Chinese wormwood, is a variant of the mugwort plant, a perennial herb belonging to the daisy family. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which help to combat infections, reduce pain and inflammation, and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Chinese wormwood include relief from digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system and improve liver function. Chinese wormwood can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in aromatherapy and as a topical application to promote wound healing.
